description_long: This is a large, rounded ceramic vase with a wide body and a slightly narrower, cylindrical neck finished in a reddish-brown color. The main body of the vase is decorated with a repeating motif of stylized fish, painted in light beige against a dark, almost black background, creating a striking contrast. The fish are arranged in horizontal rows, each fish featuring a simple, elongated form with visible eyes and fins. The surface of the vase appears matte and slightly rough, suggesting it is hand-crafted and possibly inspired by traditional or indigenous pottery styles. The interior of the vase is dark and hollow, indicating it is designed to hold objects or liquids. The overall appearance is rustic and artistic, making it suitable as a decorative piece in urban public or private spaces.
description: A large, rounded ceramic vase with a reddish-brown neck and a body decorated with rows of stylized beige fish on a dark background. The surface is matte and slightly rough, giving it a rustic, hand-crafted look.
description_view_0: The vase's front shows the fish motif most clearly, with the reddish-brown neck and the dark, patterned body in full view.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the continuity of the fish pattern, with the fish appearing slightly larger and more spaced out.
description_view_2: The fish motif remains prominent, but the curvature of the vase makes the pattern appear more compressed.
description_view_3: The fish pattern is visible but appears lighter and more faded, possibly due to lighting or wear, with the reddish neck still prominent.
